name: ubs
description: Ultimate Bug Scanner - Pre-commit static analysis for AI coding workflows. 18 detection categories, 8 languages, 4-layer analysis engine. The AI agent's quality gate.
capabilities: Scan JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Go, Rust, Java, C++, Ruby, and Swift for common bugs
Detect 18 categories of issues including null safety, security vulnerabilities, async/await problems, and memory leaks
Integrate with git pre-commit hooks and AI coding agent workflows
Generate reports in text, JSON, JSONL, SARIF, and HTML formats
Run focused scans on staged files or specific file paths in under 1 second
Compare scan results against baselines to detect regressions
author: Dicklesworthstone
securitySummary: This skill is documentation-only for the UBS static analysis tool. All 122 static findings are false positives from code examples in the markdown documentation showing bash commands and installation instructions. No executable code is present. The skill requires users to install the external UBS tool via curl, which is standard for CLI tool installation. Risk level is low due to external command dependency.
valueStatement: AI agents write code fast but can introduce bugs like null pointer errors, missing await statements, and security vulnerabilities. UBS provides pre-commit static analysis across 8 languages to catch these issues in seconds before they reach production.
userTitle: Scan Code for Bugs Before Every Commit
limitations: Requires external installation of the UBS tool and dependencies like ast-grep and ripgrep
Cannot fix bugs automatically, only detects and reports them
May produce false positives that require manual review and suppression
Performance degrades on very large codebases over 200K lines

useCases.targetUser: Solo developers and teams using AI coding assistants
useCases.title: Pre-Commit Quality Gate
useCases.description: Install UBS as a git pre-commit hook to automatically scan staged files before every commit. Exit code 1 blocks the commit until critical issues are fixed.

useCases.targetUser: DevOps engineers setting up automated testing pipelines
useCases.title: CI/CD Pipeline Integration
useCases.description: Run UBS in strict mode during continuous integration to enforce code quality standards. Generate SARIF reports for GitHub code scanning integration.

useCases.targetUser: Developers using AI coding agents for rapid prototyping
useCases.title: AI Agent Code Review
useCases.description: Configure Claude Code, Cursor, or other AI agents to automatically run UBS after file writes. Get instant feedback on null safety, security, and async issues.

promptTemplates.title: Scan Staged Files Before Commit
promptTemplates.prompt: Run UBS on my staged git files and show me any critical issues
promptTemplates.scenario: You have made changes and want to check them before committing

promptTemplates.title: Full Project Security Scan
promptTemplates.prompt: Run a complete security scan of my project using UBS and highlight any XSS, injection, or hardcoded secret issues
promptTemplates.scenario: You want to audit your entire codebase for security vulnerabilities

promptTemplates.title: Check Specific File
promptTemplates.prompt: Run UBS on src/components/UserProfile.tsx and check for null safety and async/await issues
promptTemplates.scenario: You just wrote a new component and want quick feedback

promptTemplates.title: Generate CI Report
promptTemplates.prompt: Run UBS in CI mode with JSON output and save the results to .ubs/latest.json
promptTemplates.scenario: You are setting up automated quality checks in your build pipeline

bestPractices: Run UBS on staged files only during development for fast feedback loops under 1 second
Use strict mode with fail-on-warning in CI/CD pipelines to enforce quality standards
Investigate each finding before suppressing with ubs:ignore comments to avoid masking real bugs
antiPatterns: Running full project scans on every file write instead of scoping to changed files
Ignoring all findings from a category without reviewing each instance individually
Using --no-verify to bypass pre-commit hooks instead of fixing or properly suppressing issues

faq.question: How do I install UBS on my system?
faq.answer: Run the installation command from the documentation which downloads the UBS script from GitHub. You may also need to install dependencies like ast-grep and ripgrep using ubs doctor --fix.

faq.question: Can UBS automatically fix the bugs it finds?
faq.answer: No, UBS only detects and reports bugs. You must manually review each finding and apply the suggested fix. This ensures you understand the issue and avoid incorrect automated fixes.

faq.question: How do I integrate UBS with Claude Code?
faq.answer: UBS can create a file write hook at .claude/hooks/on-file-write.sh that automatically runs scans when files are modified. You can also manually invoke UBS via prompts like run UBS on this file.

faq.question: What should I do if UBS reports too many false positives?
faq.answer: First verify each finding is truly a false positive. Then use inline suppression comments like ubs:ignore or ubs:ignore-next-line. You can also skip entire categories with the --skip flag.

faq.question: Does UBS work with all programming languages?
faq.answer: UBS supports JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Go, Rust, Java, C++, Ruby, and Swift. It will skip files in other languages during scans.

faq.question: How fast is UBS on large codebases?
faq.answer: UBS processes about 10,000 lines per second. Small projects under 5K lines scan in under 1 second. Large projects with 200K lines take about 12 seconds. Use --staged or file paths to scan only changed code.
